I am curious by nature and a storyteller at heart. Behind everything I create – a brand, a website, an image – there is one question: does this feel right?

Case study · 2025

Simbibimbis –
a brand built from scratch

Simbibimbis is my own plant-based dog treat brand, handmade in Sweden. The first product – The Good Staff – is heart-shaped treats in 80g bags made with plant-based ingredients like oats, chickpea flour, peanut butter and coconut oil. Everything – from the name and logo to the packaging, website and tone of voice – I designed and built myself. This is the story of how I thought.

01

The starting point

The brand needed to reflect a genuine story – rescue dog Simba, the little stray dog Lillstrumpa I met in India, and the conviction that every choice we make as consumers has consequences. The design had to feel warm, authentic and premium without being pretentious.

02

Visual identity

I chose kraft paper as packaging material for its natural, handcrafted feel. The colour palette was drawn directly from the material – warm browns and amber tones. The circular logo with Simba's illustration creates recognition and warmth.

03

Tone of voice

Tagline: "Life is short. Treat your dog with kindness." – a deliberate wordplay where treat means both dog treats and to treat well. The brand voice is personal, warm and honest. Storytelling rather than selling.

04

Website

Designed and built a bilingual (SV/EN) website focused on storytelling, a clear purchase journey and rescue commitment as a central part of the experience. Every section tells a part of the brand story.

05

Sustainability as design

The donation concept – 10 SEK per bag to rescue organisations – is not just an ethical choice but a designed part of the customer experience. "Rescue dog of the month" creates recurring engagement and makes every purchase meaningful.

06

Results

A cohesive brand with strong visual identity and clear values. Already before launch, the brand has organic engagement with reels reaching over 100,000 views and 4,000 shares.

Seeing the world

Approach

Feeling over perfection

About me

The storyteller
behind the brand

I am Angelica Jennesved – founder, designer and storyteller. I believe the best experiences are created when form and feeling meet.

My path towards UX design has gone through theatre, media and communication, volunteer work with rescue dogs in India and building Simbibimbis. Every step has been about the same thing: understanding people and creating experiences that move them.

I have always been interested in what makes something feel right – why a brand creates trust, why a website is easy to navigate, why an image stays in your memory. UX design is the answer to those questions.

Background

Educated in theatre and media & communication. Experience in pedagogy and guiding people through experiences as a museum educator. Strong background in storytelling, creative writing and audience-focused communication.

Commitment

Volunteer for rescue organisations. Worked with stray dogs in India. Founder of Simbibimbis – a brand built on care and responsibility.

Skills

Brand design, visual identity, storytelling, e-commerce, social media, photo and video.

Values

Committed to sustainability, animal welfare and conscious consumption. These values run through everything I create.

Why UX design

Design that starts
with the human

01

From theatre to UX

Theatre is about understanding how an experience affects a person – how time, space and movement create feeling. That is exactly what UX design does, but digitally. My theatre background has given me a unique perspective on how experiences are built.

02

Simbibimbis as a UX lab

Building Simbibimbis has been an intensive UX project – how does a customer find the right product, understand the values, feel trust and complete a purchase? Every design decision was about the user's experience.

03

Design with purpose

I want to design experiences that not only work but mean something. Accessibility, inclusion and sustainability are not constraints – they are prerequisites for good design. That is the kind of UX designer I want to become.

Let's create
something together

I am always open to conversations about design, storytelling and creating.
